notes: Author organization; Boston-based market research and consulting firm.
Group VP Aberdeen Group; author of Linuxcare profile.
Founded 1998 San Francisco CA; first enterprise Linux services company; 650 Townsend St; acquired by Levanta 2004 then faded; pioneered the enterprise Linux support model.
Lead investor in Linuxcare; cited as its first Linux-related investment. Early bet on Linux enterprise market proved visionary.
Key Linuxcare OEM partner; contracted for back-end Linux support on OptiPlex Dimension Precision workstations and PowerEdge Servers.
Key Linuxcare OEM partner; contracted for Linux support on RS/6000 line.
Key Linuxcare OEM partner; contracted for support of Star Office suite (acquired from Star Division).
Linuxcare services and support partner for North American customers; one of the major Linux distributions in 1999.
Not named in document but the direct long-term successor to Linuxcare's enterprise Linux services model; IBM acquired Red Hat for $34B in 2019 validating the market thesis.
Linuxcare President/CEO; former Cylink CEO; 23-year IBM general manager background.
Linuxcare CTO and co-founder; contributed to GNU Emacs and Linux kernel; later founded Technorati.
Linux guru at Linuxcare; original author of Samba (SMB/CIFS implementation for Linux/Unix); one of the most influential open source contributors.
Linuxcare VP Service Solutions; 18 years at DEC (Digital Equipment Corporation).
Former employer of Pat Lambs (VP Service Solutions); major Unix/VAX workstation and server vendor acquired by Compaq.
Certified DeskPro desktops with Linuxcare; early enterprise Linux hardware certification partner.
Developer of StarOffice acquired by Sun in 1999; Linuxcare provided Linux support for Star Office; StarOffice later became OpenOffice.org.
